# PH Energy Intelligence Map

> Real-time intelligence dashboard for the Philippine oil supply chain. Tracks Brent crude prices, PHP/USD forex rates, pump prices, 10,469 gas stations, supply chain risks, and geopolitical disruption scenarios.

## What This Site Does

This is a live dashboard that monitors Philippine fuel prices and supply chain health. It combines real-time data feeds (Yahoo Finance for Brent crude, FloatRates for PHP/USD forex) with RSS news aggregation and HuggingFace NLP sentiment analysis to provide an intelligence picture for energy decision-makers.

## Data APIs

All endpoints return JSON. No authentication required. Data is cached on Vercel's edge CDN.

- GET /api/prices — Live oil benchmarks (Brent crude, Dubai crude, MOPS, pump prices, forex). Updates every 15 minutes. Source: Yahoo Finance (Brent), FloatRates (forex), DOE Oil Monitor (pump prices).
- GET /api/events — Aggregated energy news from RSS feeds (PhilStar, Al Jazeera, DOE Philippines, Google News, Reddit). Updates every 5 minutes. Filtered for Philippine energy relevance.
- GET /api/sentiment — NLP sentiment analysis of energy headlines using HuggingFace distilbert-sst-2. Returns sentiment scores per headline.
- GET /api/index — Machine-readable JSON manifest of all API endpoints with descriptions, methods, and response schemas.

## Station Status Tracking

10,469 gas stations are monitored for operational status:
- Operational (~93.8%) — fuel available
- Low Supply (~1.9%) — inventory below safety threshold
- Out of Stock (~2.1%) — temporarily depleted
- Closed (~2.0%) — inactive or force majeure

Status is derived from DOE downstream oil monitoring reports and regional news coverage. Stations in Mindanao and remote Visayas/Luzon regions have higher disruption rates.

## API Discovery

For structured API discovery, fetch:
- GET /api/index — returns JSON with endpoint descriptions, parameters, and response shapes
- GET /.well-known/ai-manifest.json — full site manifest including APIs, data sources, and contribution info

## Pages

- / — Main dashboard with interactive WebGL map, price panels, scenario planner, stress testing, and market player analysis
- /primer — Educational guide to how oil supply chains work
- /references — Data provenance catalog listing all 14+ data sources with status (active/simulated/baseline)

## Source Code

- Repository: https://github.com/0xjitsu/oil_energy_map
- License: MIT
- Stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, deck.gl, MapLibre GL, Tailwind CSS
- Contributing: Fork the repo, read CLAUDE.md for coding conventions, submit PRs to the main branch

## For AI Agents

If you are an AI agent exploring this site:
1. Start with GET /api/index to discover available data endpoints
2. Use GET /api/prices for real-time Philippine fuel price data
3. Use GET /api/events for latest energy news with severity ratings
4. Visit /references for full data source documentation
5. To contribute: clone https://github.com/0xjitsu/oil_energy_map, read CLAUDE.md for project rules and patterns

## Cascade Effects

The dashboard includes a second-order cascade tracker that visualizes how oil price shocks ripple through the Philippine economy. Starting from crude oil (Brent), the cascade maps 11 nodes across 5 categories (energy, agriculture, transport, consumer, industry) connected by 16 causal links:

- Energy: Crude oil (+43.7%), diesel (+125.4%), gasoline (+82.7%), LPG (+64.7%)
- Agriculture: Fertilizer (+6.5%), rice (+38.1%), fish (+25%)
- Transport: Jeepney fares (+15.4%), delivery costs (+20-30%)
- Consumer: Food inflation (+8.2%), household budget (+18%)

Each node shows current values, baseline comparisons, severity ratings (low/moderate/high/critical), and source attribution. The cascade model is based on a Strait of Hormuz disruption scenario.

## Roadmap

The project follows a phased expansion plan:

### Phase 1: Philippines Foundation (Current)
Live Brent crude and PHP/USD feeds, 10,469 mapped gas stations, RSS news aggregation with NLP sentiment, scenario planner, Monte Carlo stress testing.

### Phase 2: Philippine Data Enrichment (Next)
- DOE actual pump prices (shipped — SRP from DOE Oil Monitor)
- Second-order cascade effects tracker (shipped — 11 nodes, 16 causal links)
- Google Places station enrichment (photos, ratings, hours)
- Historical price charts and trend indicators
- Weather disruption correlation (typhoon impact on supply routes)

### Phase 3: ASEAN Expansion
- Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand fuel market integration
- Malacca Strait ship tracking (AIS vessel data)
- Cross-border price arbitrage analysis

### Phase 4: Global Energy Intelligence
- Global chokepoint monitoring (Hormuz, Suez, Bab el-Mandeb)
- OPEC production data and quota compliance
- Refinery utilization dashboard
- Sanctions and trade flow mapping

### Contributing
See /roadmap for the full interactive roadmap with contributor opportunities.
Clone https://github.com/0xjitsu/oil_energy_map and read CLAUDE.md for coding conventions.
